title: Sikaflex Mortar Fix, Limestone, Polyurethane sealant for repairing damaged mortar, joints and gaps. Sealing mortar cracks 10.1 fl. oz Cartridge
description:
About this item
Item Weight: 1.0 lb
Country of Origin: China
Brand name: SIKA
Item Dimensions: 1.85"L x 1.85"W x 11.5"H
reviews_5s:
Works great!I bought the one that is colored limestone, but it looks silver next to my mortar. I'll take another picture in a week or so once it's had time to cure to see if it changes colors.

Either way, it is exactly the color that is shown from the manufacturer in the pictures when it comes out of the tube.workedEasy to use.This is just the right texture & consistency for caulking the limestone of my house. There were hairline cracks in the limestone where I could see daylight from the garage to outside. Filling the cracks horizontally is easy with this product as it doesn’t easily leak out. I used this product name for self leveling sidewalks & the driveway also. Very happy with this brand. Be sure to use something such as paper towel or rag to catch the flow till you get it in the upright position. Otherwise quite messy.Great product.
I have used this in several different applications.
Extremely durable and flexible.
Use a wet finger to smooth out your joint.
It is paintable if necessary.
When using on a finished surface (visible and not paintable), throw play sand on the joint while sticky. This will help blend with the existing mortar.Took longer than the tube said to cure but now that it's all set, it's all good now!Mix black and white clay together, you get gray clay. That's what this looks like. Dries flexible, like thick rubber. Does not look like sand finish, more like a smooth cement finish. I would not use it for brick without adding sand into the finish. Look for videos on how to install this correctly. (tape edges, apply, press in to smooth, spray soapy water to control to finish etc.) You should also just try one tube to see what you are getting, before buying many tubes. (best product for filling the control joints in patio or sidewalk cracks, again look for videos to learn the technic for a pro finish)Works As AdvertisedLook at this as a grey version of construction adhesive. It works very well.
reviews_1s:
This product is very difficult to squeeze out of the tube. Great force must be applied to squeeze out a small drop. In addition, you must wear gloves as it is difficult to remove if it gets on you or anything else. I ended up throwing away my caulking gun as it got covered with this stuff. This stuff looks white and didn't blend in with other mortar. I ended up removing everything I applied and doing the job with Quickcrete Mortar. Much better.The product could not be dispensed. I had to cut the bottom of the tube off and scoop out the sealant and apply with a putty knife. As I moved further up the tube, the sealant became stiffer and less fluid. The very top of the tube was not spreadable. Very poor quality control and a frustrating product to use.From the day it arrived it was very diffucult to get it to come out of the tube...and yes it was well punctured and cut off a large hole on tip
I have many years experience using various forms of materials for caulking guns...you have to have a very strong grip to get it to moveUnbelievable how difficult this was to dispense. I have have used every kind of caulk and mortar repair with my over 35 years as a homeowner and nothing has been as difficult as this.
I couldn't believe it.. When I finally got some in the crack.. As I pulled the nozzle tip out.. The product came out with it in a weird clumpy stingy mess.
It is so sticky like silicone and glue mix.
It would have been easier to mix mortar.
The person that said it was like chewing gum is absolutely right.I'm going to spend my lunch break making an unexpected run to Home Depot to buy a replacement for this junk.
1) Provide a straw, wire, pipe to puncture the seal, this use your product
2) Improve the consistency of your product. The product has the consistency of chewed gum. It sticks to itself, not the surfaces that it's supposed to get applied to. Can't fill mortar holes or spread easily.Great product, but it simply did not discharge using a caulking gun. I cut a wide hole, and no amount of squeezing would get more than a small amount out. I wound up cutting open the tube and applying it with a putty knife and fingers.even after I pierced the foil this is so hard to get out of the tube. I had my neighbor try as well and he had to use both hands to try to get it to move.we did not like this and have removed it from the patio. it stayed soft like caulk, it bubbled and the color was not great. we would not purchase this again.I bought these because they say it's limestone color, kind of whitish/beiges for stone houses, but when I open the first one, for my surprise, it's color gray, the same as all other products but they say straight out it's color gray. I was so happy to find a stone color to fix some of my house mortar. I cannot use them where I need it. So disappointed.I expected this mortar repair to be limestone volor, not cement grey.
